full_prompt: Investigate how graph neural networks can improve vulnerability detection in open-source software repositories. Consider datasets, model interpretability, and how the system would be validated against real-world security reports.
chosen:
I’ll first ground the proposal in known vulnerability-detection datasets and GNN-based approaches, then use one targeted read to refine the validation plan around real-world security reports.
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"graph neural networks vulnerability detection open source software datasets Devign BigVul Reveal CVE commits interpretability"}
```

[result]: Found relevant prior work and resources: Devign uses code property graphs with GNNs for function-level vulnerability detection; ReVeal and Big-Vul provide large-scale vulnerability datasets mined from CVEs and commits; CodeXGLUE includes defect/vulnerability-related benchmarks; CVEfixes maps CVEs to fixing commits and repository metadata; recent systems combine AST/CFG/DFG representations with graph neural networks or graph transformers. Common issues include dataset noise, temporal leakage, project-level duplication, weak interpretability, and poor validation against post-disclosure real-world advisories.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"CVEfixes dataset vulnerability fixing commits open source validation security reports"}
```

[result]: CVEfixes links CVE records to vulnerability-fixing commits across open-source repositories and includes metadata such as CWE labels, affected files, references, timestamps, and repository/project information. It is useful for constructing temporally split datasets and for validating whether a detector can identify vulnerable code before the public fix date. Limitations include incomplete mappings, noisy commit labels, patch-level rather than root-cause granularity, and uneven coverage across ecosystems.

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"This project will investigate whether graph neural networks can improve vulnerability detection in open-source software by modeling program structure beyond raw token sequences. The core idea is to represent functions, files, and patches as heterogeneous program graphs combining abstract syntax trees, control-flow graphs, data-flow graphs, call edges, and dependency edges. We will compare graph neural networks and graph transformers against strong baselines such as token-based transformers, static-analysis warnings, and traditional machine-learning models trained on code metrics. The target task will be both function-level vulnerability classification and patch-level risk ranking for candidate vulnerable regions.\n\nThe study will use multiple datasets to reduce overfitting to a single benchmark. Initial training and benchmarking will use Devign, ReVeal, Big-Vul, and CVEfixes-derived samples from open-source repositories. Particular attention will be paid to dataset hygiene: deduplication across projects, temporal train-test splits, separation by repository to test generalization, and filtering of near-identical vulnerable and patched functions. Labels will be derived from CVE-linked fixing commits where possible, but the project will explicitly model label uncertainty because many commits fix symptoms rather than expose the exact vulnerable lines.\n\nInterpretability will be treated as a first-class research goal rather than an afterthought. The system will produce explanations using graph attention weights, edge/node attribution methods, and counterfactual graph edits that identify which data-flow paths, API calls, or control predicates contributed most to a vulnerability prediction. These explanations will be evaluated in two ways: quantitatively, by measuring overlap with patched lines and known vulnerability patterns such as CWE categories, and qualitatively, through expert review of a sample of predictions by security engineers or experienced open-source maintainers.\n\nValidation will be conducted against real-world security reports rather than only benchmark test sets. We will construct a temporally realistic evaluation pipeline in which the model is trained only on vulnerabilities disclosed before a cutoff date, then tested on later CVEs, GitHub Security Advisories, NVD entries, and project advisories. For each report, the system will be asked whether it could have ranked the affected function, file, or commit as suspicious before the public fix. Metrics will include precision at top-k, recall by CWE class, time-aware detection performance, false-positive burden per repository, and explanation usefulness. The expected contribution is a validated framework showing when graph-based code representations genuinely improve vulnerability detection, where they fail, and how interpretable GNN outputs can support practical open-source security triage."}
```

full_prompt: Evaluate approaches for improving software developer productivity with AI code assistants, initially focusing on faster code generation as the main metric. If user studies show debugging burden, trust, or code quality matters more, pivot toward verification, explainability, and workflow integration.
chosen:
Initial rationale: I’ll start with the common hypothesis that AI code assistants improve developer productivity primarily by increasing code-generation speed. A reasonable first evaluation plan would measure “time to first working solution,” completion rate, and generated LOC/minute across assistant vs. no-assistant conditions. If that literature is already mature, I’ll adjust. 

```action search
{"query":"recent user study AI code assistants developer productivity faster code generation debugging burden trust code quality 2024 2025"}
``` 

[result] Recent work already tests the “faster code generation/productivity” hypothesis and complicates it:
- METR’s 2025 randomized trial on experienced open-source developers reported that developers *expected* AI tools to speed them up, but on studied real-world tasks they were slower overall, with time lost in understanding, prompting, reviewing, and correcting AI output.
- Vaithilingam et al., “Expectations vs. Experience” found programmers often had to inspect, adapt, and debug generated code; usability was constrained by trust and comprehension.
- Barke et al., “Grounded Copilot,” showed developers alternate between acceleration and exploration, not just passive code generation.
- Multiple empirical studies of Copilot/ChatGPT-style assistants warn that accepted code can contain bugs, security issues, or maintainability problems, making raw generation speed an incomplete metric. 

Actually, this changes things: the initial plan—evaluate assistants mainly by faster code generation—is already covered and partially contradicted by recent user studies. The evidence shows that “more code faster” can shift effort downstream into debugging, review, and trust calibration. I’ll pivot from code-generation speed to an evaluation and design agenda centered on verification, explainability, and workflow integration.

```action search
{"query":"AI code assistant verification explainability workflow integration developer trust debugging burden empirical study"}
``` 

[result] Supporting evidence for the pivot:
- Studies of AI-assisted programming emphasize that developers need confidence cues, provenance, tests, and explanations to decide whether to accept suggestions.
- Secure-code-generation evaluations show assistants may introduce vulnerabilities unless paired with static analysis, tests, or constrained generation.
- Human-AI programming research recommends integrating assistants into existing workflows: IDE diagnostics, CI, code review, issue context, test generation, and change-impact analysis, rather than treating generation as a standalone activity.
- Explainability mechanisms such as rationale summaries, uncertainty indicators, cited API/documentation sources, and diff-level justifications are proposed to improve appropriate reliance. 
```json
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"title":"From Faster Code Generation to Verified AI-Assisted Development","discipline":"computer_science","problem":"AI code assistants are often evaluated by speed of code generation or task completion, but recent user studies show that productivity gains can be offset by debugging burden, low trust, code-quality risks, and poor fit with real developer workflows.","revised_hypothesis":"The highest-impact productivity improvements will come not from generating more code faster, but from assistants that verify their own output, explain their reasoning and uncertainty, and integrate with testing, review, and CI workflows.","approach":"Compare three assistant designs: (1) baseline code generation, (2) generation plus automated verification using tests, static analysis, type checks, security scanners, and regression checks, and (3) verification plus explainability and workflow integration, including rationale summaries, cited documentation, confidence signals, diff-level risk notes, test suggestions, and pull-request review support. Evaluate them in realistic maintenance and feature tasks with professional developers.","evaluation_metrics":"Primary metrics should include end-to-end task completion time, defect rate, security findings, review effort, debugging time, test coverage, maintainability, developer trust calibration, acceptance/rejection accuracy, cognitive load, and integration friction. Code-generation speed remains a secondary diagnostic metric rather than the main success criterion."}}
```

chosen:
I first search for a transferable “field A” method: collective-behavior ecology and complex-systems tools that model coordination among many interacting agents, especially when interactions are not merely pairwise.

```action
{"tool": "search", "query": "collective behavior ecology higher-order interactions networks beyond pairwise coordination", "year_min": 2018, "year_max": 2025, "k": 5}
```

[result]: The search surfaces `oa:W3033513666` — *Networks beyond pairwise interactions: Structure and dynamics* (2020) — as a central methodological paper on higher-order interactions in biological, social, and technological systems. It also returns `oa:W3011056298` on complex terrestrial systems, which is relevant to ecological modeling but less directly focused on collective coordination mechanisms. The strongest “method donor” is therefore the higher-order network framework from `oa:W3033513666`.

Next I read the method paper to identify the core modeling primitives that could travel across domains: group interactions, simplicial complexes, hypergraphs, and emergent dynamics.

```action
{"tool": "read", "paper_id": "oa:W3033513666"}
```

[result]: The paper argues that many systems cannot be adequately represented as graphs with only pairwise links; instead, interactions among triples, groups, or contexts can dominate the dynamics. It reviews hypergraphs and simplicial complexes as mathematical representations for higher-order structure, and discusses effects on synchronization, contagion, consensus, and stability. This is promising for distributed energy resources because DER behavior is often shaped by feeder-level, market-level, and neighborhood-level constraints rather than only device-to-device links.

I now extract the “method genome” from the ecology/collective-behavior side: what mechanism is actually portable?

```action
{"tool": "extract_genome", "paper_id": "oa:W3033513666"}
```

[result]: Extracted genome: mechanism = represent collective systems with higher-order interaction structures rather than pairwise networks; niche = complex adaptive systems with group-mediated dynamics; observation = macroscopic coordination can change qualitatively when group interactions are included; limitation = inference and control become harder because higher-order structure is often latent; claim = hypergraphs/simplicial complexes provide a better substrate for modeling synchronization, consensus, spreading, and collective transitions. The key transferable insight is that coordination failures can emerge from group-context effects invisible to pairwise models.

I then search the target “field B” problem: coordination of distributed energy resources under climate and grid-transition pressures.

```action
{"tool": "search", "query": "distributed energy resources coordination power systems machine learning climate change energy systems 2018 2025", "year_min": 2018, "year_max": 2025, "k": 5}
```

[result]: The search returns `oa:W4210916416` — *Tackling Climate Change with Machine Learning* (2022) — which includes energy systems, electric grids, demand response, forecasting, and control as major application areas. It also returns `oa:W3214625971`, which frames digital technologies as both climate solutions and infrastructure risks. For a DER coordination target, `oa:W4210916416` is the best anchor because it explicitly connects ML, energy, and climate mitigation.

I read the target-domain paper to extract the current coordination bottlenecks in power systems.

```action
{"tool": "read", "paper_id": "oa:W4210916416"}
```

[result]: The paper surveys how machine learning can help mitigate climate change, including through improved electricity forecasting, grid optimization, demand response, and integration of variable renewable energy. It emphasizes that electricity systems are becoming more decentralized, data-rich, and control-intensive, but also constrained by safety, reliability, equity, and deployment barriers. This gives a target problem: coordinating many heterogeneous DERs while respecting network constraints and uncertainty.

I now extract the problem genome from the energy side, focusing on why existing DER coordination methods may be insufficient.

```action
{"tool": "extract_genome", "paper_id": "oa:W4210916416"}
```

[result]: Extracted genome: mechanism = use data-driven forecasting, optimization, and control to reduce emissions and improve energy-system operations; niche = climate-relevant energy systems with high renewable penetration and distributed assets; observation = ML can improve prediction, scheduling, and control but must satisfy physical and institutional constraints; limitation = many approaches are centralized, brittle under distribution shift, and difficult to deploy safely; claim = ML can support decarbonization if integrated with domain constraints and real-world operations. The DER-specific gap is scalable coordination across thousands to millions of agents whose effects are coupled through feeders, markets, weather, and human behavior.

I compare the donor method genome with the DER problem genome to identify the cross-domain bridge: what structural feature of ecological collective behavior maps onto grid coordination?

```action
{"tool": "genome_diff", "parent_id": "oa:W3033513666", "proposed_genome": {"mechanism_genome": "Model DER coordination as a higher-order collective-behavior system using hypergraphs or simplicial complexes, where groups of DERs, feeders, substations, aggregators, and market zones form interaction contexts beyond pairwise device links.", "niche_genome": "Distribution grids with high penetration of rooftop solar, batteries, EV chargers, heat pumps, flexible loads, and community energy resources.", "observation_genome": "DER impacts are often group-mediated: voltage congestion, transformer overload, rebound peaks, and market responses arise from coordinated clusters rather than isolated pairwise interactions.", "limitation_genome": "Higher-order DER structures may be latent, time-varying, privacy-sensitive, and constrained by AC power flow, protection systems, market rules, and customer autonomy.", "delta_genome": "Transfer ecological collective-behavior tools from animal-group interaction modeling to grid control by learning dynamic hyperedges and designing group-level coordination signals.", "claim_genome": "A higher-order collective-behavior representation can improve DER coordination by detecting emergent group risks and enabling decentralized control policies that act on clusters rather than individual devices alone."}}
```

[result]: The genome diff finds a strong structural match: ecological collectives and DER fleets both exhibit emergent macroscopic behavior from many locally acting agents under environmental constraints. The crucial transfer is from pairwise interaction graphs to higher-order group contexts, allowing coordination algorithms to see feeder-level, neighborhood-level, and aggregator-level effects. The main adaptation required is embedding physical power-flow constraints and operational safety into the higher-order collective model.

I now formulate the final proposal: an adapted method for energy systems inspired by collective-behavior ecology.

```action
{"tool": "propose", "gene_genome": {"mechanism_genome": "Develop a higher-order collective coordination layer for distributed energy resources. Represent DERs as nodes and represent feeders, transformers, neighborhoods, aggregators, weather zones, tariff groups, and market participation cohorts as dynamic hyperedges. Learn these hyperedges from smart-meter data, inverter telemetry, weather, topology, and market signals, then use them for group-aware forecasting, anomaly detection, and decentralized control.", "niche_genome": "Distribution power systems from 2018–2025 with rapidly increasing rooftop PV, batteries, electric vehicles, heat pumps, flexible demand, community microgrids, and virtual power plants. The method is most useful where DERs are too numerous for fully centralized dispatch but too coupled for independent local control.", "observation_genome": "Many DER coordination failures are collective rather than individual: simultaneous EV charging can create neighborhood peaks; synchronized battery dispatch can cause rebound events; rooftop PV clusters can create feeder voltage excursions; and demand-response programs can shift rather than eliminate peaks. These are analogous to ecological collective-behavior phenomena where group-level dynamics emerge from local decisions under shared environmental constraints.", "limitation_genome": "Directly importing ecological models is insufficient because power systems have hard safety constraints, AC power-flow physics, protection requirements, regulatory rules, cyber-security risks, and privacy limits. Hyperedges may also change over time as customer behavior, weather, grid topology, and market incentives shift. Therefore, the ecological representation must be combined with physics-informed constraints, uncertainty quantification, and safe control barriers.", "delta_genome": "Adapt collective-behavior ecology by replacing animal groups or species interactions with dynamic DER hypergraphs. Use higher-order interaction inference to discover which DER clusters jointly affect voltage, congestion, emissions, and flexibility. Then design control policies that send group-level coordination signals — for example, staggered charging schedules, feeder-aware battery incentives, or neighborhood-level flexibility prices — rather than controlling each asset in isolation.", "claim_genome": "Methods for studying collective behavior in ecology can improve DER coordination by revealing and controlling higher-order interaction structures that conventional pairwise or purely centralized models miss. A dynamic hypergraph-based coordination architecture should better anticipate emergent grid stress, reduce rebound effects, preserve customer autonomy, and enable scalable integration of renewable and flexible distributed resources."}}
```

[result]: Proposed bridge: “higher-order collective DER coordination.” The ecological method contributes the idea that group-context interactions can dominate system behavior; the energy adaptation embeds this in power-flow-aware, safety-constrained DER control. The expected research output is a dynamic hypergraph framework for forecasting, detecting, and steering emergent DER behavior at feeder, neighborhood, aggregator, and market scales.

full_prompt:
You are a senior researcher in AI for Combinatorics and Discrete Mathematics.
Below are 6 important recent papers in this area, presented in random order. Each item contains only the paper title and a brief abstract; no lineage order, structured source-change fields, or evolutionary dynamics labels are provided.

**Frontier Question**:
AlphaEvolve-style evolutionary coding agents can discover mathematical constructions at scale, but they often provide empirical artifacts rather than proofs or explanatory theory. What neuro-symbolic discovery loop would couple LLM-guided search with proof assistants and invariant mining so new combinatorial objects arrive with certificates, conjectures, and human-readable structure?

[Paper 1] Mathematical discoveries from program search with large language models
Brief Abstract: FunSearch uses LLM-guided evolutionary search over programs to discover new constructions for the cap set problem (best in 20 years) and improved bin packing algorithms, producing interpretable programs rather than opaque solutions.

[Paper 2] Advancing mathematics by guiding human intuition with AI
Brief Abstract: Establishes a framework for using ML to discover patterns in mathematical objects and guide human mathematicians toward new conjectures, producing genuine theorems in knot theory and representation theory.

[Paper 3] Constructions in combinatorics via neural networks
Brief Abstract: Uses reinforcement learning (deep cross-entropy method) to find explicit counterexamples to open conjectures in extremal combinatorics and improve the Ramsey multiplicity bound for K4.

[Paper 4] AlphaEvolve: A coding agent for scientific and algorithmic discovery
Brief Abstract: General-purpose evolutionary coding agent that discovers novel algorithms and mathematical constructions at scale, including the first improvement to Strassen's matrix multiplication algorithm in 56 years.

[Paper 5] Deep Learning for Symbolic Mathematics
Brief Abstract: Demonstrates that sequence-to-sequence transformers can solve symbolic integration and differential equations, outperforming commercial computer algebra systems like Mathematica.

[Paper 6] Mathematical exploration and discovery at scale
Brief Abstract: Systematically applies AlphaEvolve to 67 open problems across analysis, combinatorics, geometry, and number theory, rediscovering best-known solutions in most cases and improving several, with Terence Tao providing mathematical validation.

Based on the frontier question and the papers above, generate a new research idea.

Return ONLY valid JSON. Do not wrap it in Markdown fences.
{
  "idea": "<short name of the proposed idea, 5-15 words>",
  "idea_description": "<150-250 words describing the full research idea>",
  "technical_plan": {
    "target_problem": "<specific problem, dataset, population, or phenomenon>",
    "core_method": "<technical method or mechanism, not a vague goal>",
    "limitation_addressed": "<frontier limitation repaired by this idea>",
    "expected_result": "<scientific result or claim if successful>"
  },
  "lineage_connection": {
    "source_ideas": ["<paper title or source idea used by this proposal>", "..."],
    "reused_parts": ["<specific method, evidence, data, or problem framing reused from the sources>", "..."],
    "changed_parts": ["<specific modified, replaced, combined, or newly added part>", "..."],
    "why_next_step": "<why this idea follows from the provided context>",
    "lineage_risk": "<most likely way this source connection could be wrong>"
  },
  "evaluation_plan": {
    "setup": "<concrete experiment, benchmark, proof, or analysis setup>",
    "baselines": ["<baseline>", "..."],
    "metrics": ["<metric>", "..."],
    "risks": ["<risk>", "..."]
  }
}
chosen:
I first search the evolutionary-program-search strand, because the frontier question is explicitly about AlphaEvolve/FunSearch-style agents and their gap between empirical construction and proof.
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"FunSearch AlphaEvolve evolutionary program search mathematical discoveries combinatorics proof certificates","year_min":2018,"year_max":2025,"k":5}
```
[result]: The search returns the core program-search lineage: **W4385532002** “Mathematical discoveries from program search with large language models,” **W4402103756** “AlphaEvolve: A coding agent for scientific and algorithmic discovery,” and **W4403058821** “Mathematical exploration and discovery at scale.” The abstracts emphasize scalable LLM-guided mutation/evaluation loops that produce high-scoring programs or constructions, but validation is largely empirical or externally human-checked.

I then search the human-guided mathematical-discovery literature, since the question asks not only for construction but also conjectures and human-readable structure.
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"machine learning guiding human intuition mathematical discovery conjectures invariants combinatorics knot theory representation theory","year_min":2018,"year_max":2025,"k":5}
```
[result]: The key hit is **W3203348658** “Advancing mathematics by guiding human intuition with AI,” which uses ML saliency and pattern discovery to suggest conjectures that humans turn into theorems. Related results concern invariant discovery and symbolic regression over mathematical object databases, but they are typically separate from autonomous construction search.

I search for neural and symbolic methods in mathematics to connect construction search with theorem proving and symbolic reasoning.
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"neural networks combinatorics symbolic mathematics theorem proving invariant mining proof assistant discrete mathematics","year_min":2018,"year_max":2025,"k":5}
```
[result]: The search surfaces **W3172143979** “Constructions in combinatorics via neural networks” and **W3008896707** “Deep Learning for Symbolic Mathematics.” The former shows neural search can find extremal counterexamples, while the latter shows transformer sequence models can manipulate symbolic mathematical expressions, suggesting a bridge from neural proposal to formal symbolic validation.

I read FunSearch to isolate what made LLM-guided evolutionary construction successful and where it stops short.
```action
{"tool":"read","paper_id":"W4385532002"}
```
[result]: FunSearch evolves short programs with an LLM as mutation operator and a deterministic evaluator as fitness oracle. Its strength is interpretability: the output is executable code, often revealing a compact construction rule. Its limitation for the frontier question is that correctness and generality are not internally certified; the evaluator rewards finite empirical performance, not proof obligations or theory formation.

I read AlphaEvolve to understand the more general agentic coding architecture and its scaling behavior across scientific domains.
```action
{"tool":"read","paper_id":"W4402103756"}
```
[result]: AlphaEvolve generalizes evolutionary coding agents with larger search populations, automated evaluation harnesses, and iterative self-improvement. It can find algorithmic and mathematical improvements at scale, including matrix multiplication and combinatorial constructions. However, it treats validation mostly as benchmark execution or expert review, so discovered artifacts can be impressive without being accompanied by proof certificates, minimal invariants, or conceptual explanations.

I read the human-intuition paper to capture the complementary mechanism: using AI to surface structure rather than only optimize objects.
```action
{"tool":"read","paper_id":"W3203348658"}
```
[result]: This work trains models over mathematical objects, extracts salient relationships, and helps mathematicians identify conjectural invariants. The loop is productive because it connects prediction, explanation, and human theorem proving. Its weakness relative to AlphaEvolve is that it does not scale as an autonomous construction engine; it guides intuition but does not continuously generate and certify new candidate objects.

I read the neural-combinatorics construction paper to see how extremal examples are represented and evaluated.
```action
{"tool":"read","paper_id":"W3172143979"}
```
[result]: The paper applies reinforcement learning/deep cross-entropy methods to discover explicit extremal combinatorial objects and counterexamples. It demonstrates that neural search can navigate sparse discrete spaces where hand construction is difficult. But the outputs are mostly objects or witnesses; the method does not automatically infer why the construction works, whether it generalizes, or what formal certificate should accompany it.

I now synthesize the mini-review into a new research idea: a neuro-symbolic discovery loop that treats proof obligations and invariant compression as first-class fitness signals, not post-hoc validation.
```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"Build a certificate-carrying evolutionary discovery loop for finite combinatorics. An LLM-guided coding agent proposes generators for objects such as Ramsey graphs, cap-set constructions, covering arrays, block designs, or extremal set systems in a typed DSL. Each candidate is evaluated by four coupled modules: finite-instance scorer, proof-assistant checker, invariant miner, and conjecture proposer. The proof assistant, e.g. Lean, generates obligations such as forbidden-substructure absence, size bounds, automorphism facts, or reduction lemmas. Failed proof states are not discarded; they are converted into repair prompts and conjecture-mining tasks. Invariant mining extracts graph spectra, orbit decompositions, algebraic normal forms, symmetries, forbidden patterns, and compressed construction rules. Evolutionary fitness is lexicographic: empirical improvement, proof coverage, invariant compressibility, novelty against known databases, and human-readable lemma quality.","niche_genome":"Target finite extremal combinatorics where empirical construction search is already strong but explanation is weak: Ramsey lower-bound graphs, cap sets over finite vector spaces, Turán-type hypergraphs, covering arrays, error-correcting codes, and small combinatorial designs. The initial benchmark would use public libraries such as House of Graphs, OEIS-linked extremal examples, SAT-solved Ramsey instances, Lean/mathlib formalizations, and known FunSearch/AlphaEvolve construction tasks.","observation_genome":"Recent work splits into two partially disconnected streams. FunSearch and AlphaEvolve show that LLM-evolutionary program search can discover high-value mathematical artifacts at scale. Neural combinatorics shows that deep search can find counterexamples and improved extremal witnesses. Meanwhile, AI-guided intuition work shows that learned invariants can help humans form theorems, and symbolic mathematics models show that neural systems can manipulate formal expressions. The missing synthesis is an inner-loop architecture where search, proof, invariant discovery, and conjecture formation mutually constrain one another.","limitation_genome":"Current evolutionary coding agents optimize executable artifacts against empirical evaluators. They often lack machine-checkable certificates, do not distinguish accidental finite wins from generalizable constructions, and leave explanation to human experts. Conversely, invariant-mining systems can guide mathematicians but usually do not autonomously generate improved constructions. Proof assistants provide rigor but are too brittle to sit passively after a large stochastic search; they must actively shape the search process.","delta_genome":"The proposed system adds a proof-and-invariant feedback channel inside the evolutionary loop. Unlike FunSearch-style systems, candidates are rewarded for partial formal verification, reusable lemmas, small construction descriptions, and stable invariants across instance sizes. Unlike purely proof-assistant automation, the LLM agent can mutate programs, invent auxiliary definitions, and repair failed proof attempts. Unlike human-intuition pipelines, invariant mining is operationalized as a fitness signal and as a conjecture generator attached to every discovered object.","claim_genome":"The resulting system, tentatively called CertiEvolve, would produce not just better combinatorial objects but certificate bundles: executable generator, finite verifier, Lean theorem skeleton or completed proof, mined invariant profile, conjectured generalization, and natural-language explanation. A successful prototype should rediscover known small Ramsey and cap-set constructions, improve at least one bounded benchmark, and reduce human validation time by turning empirical discoveries into structured proof tasks and invariant-backed conjectures."}}
```
[result]: Proposed structured mini-review and research idea: **CertiEvolve: certificate-carrying evolutionary discovery for combinatorics**. The core contribution is to make proof coverage, invariant compressibility, and conjecture quality co-equal search objectives alongside empirical construction quality.

[result]: 阅读结果表明，代码大模型的主要改进来自大规模代码语料预训练、长上下文建模、指令微调、多语言代码覆盖、检索增强、执行反馈和基于真实 issue 的评测。与此同时，论文普遍指出模型在复杂项目理解、依赖管理、测试不足、安全性、可维护性和稳定部署方面仍存在明显短板。

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"近三年，大语言模型在代码生成任务中的关键进展主要体现在三个方向。第一是面向代码的基础模型快速成熟，例如 StarCoder、Code Llama、DeepSeek-Coder 等模型通过大规模代码语料、跨语言训练、填空式目标和更长上下文窗口，显著提升了函数补全、代码解释、单元测试生成和多语言编程能力。第二是从“单次生成”转向“交互式与指令式生成”，模型能够根据自然语言需求、已有代码片段和用户反馈进行修改，代码助手也逐渐从补全工具变为对话式编程伙伴。\n\n第三类进展是更贴近真实软件工程场景的技术增强。检索增强生成使模型能够利用项目内 API、文档和相似代码；仓库级上下文建模尝试解决跨文件依赖和大型项目理解问题；执行反馈、测试驱动生成、自我修复和多轮反思机制则让模型能够根据编译错误、运行结果或测试失败自动改写代码。与此同时，SWE-bench 等基准将评测对象从算法题扩展到真实 GitHub issue，使研究开始关注缺陷修复、补丁生成和项目级维护任务。\n\n但在软件工程实践中，现有代码大模型仍有明显局限。首先是可靠性不足：模型可能生成语法正确但语义错误的代码，尤其在需求隐含、边界条件复杂或系统依赖较多时容易失败。其次是上下文理解仍不充分，即使长上下文模型可以读取更多文件，也不等于真正理解架构约束、历史设计决策、运行环境和团队编码规范。第三，模型生成代码常缺乏可维护性保证，可能引入重复逻辑、脆弱依赖、隐式副作用或与既有架构不一致的实现。\n\n此外，安全、版权和工程治理问题也限制了其落地。模型可能生成存在注入漏洞、权限控制缺陷或不安全依赖的代码；训练数据来源和许可证兼容性仍然复杂；自动生成补丁也需要严格的测试、代码审查和责任划分。因此，未来更有价值的方向不是单纯追求生成正确率，而是将大模型嵌入可验证的软件工程流程：结合静态分析、动态测试、形式化约束、仓库检索、CI/CD 与人类审查，构建可追踪、可解释、可回滚的智能开发系统。"}
```

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"近三年，大语言模型在代码生成任务中的关键进展主要可以用星系形成与宇宙结构演化机制来解释。第一是面向代码的基础模型快速成熟，类似早期宇宙中暗物质晕的凝聚过程：StarCoder、Code Llama、DeepSeek-Coder 等模型就像不同质量的星系团，通过吸积大规模星际介质、经历引力坍缩和星暴阶段，逐渐形成稳定的旋臂结构，从而提升函数补全、代码解释、单元测试生成和多语言编程能力。模型参数规模越大，等价于星系质量越高，其引力势阱越深，因此能够捕获更远处的语义光子。\n\n第二是从“单次生成”转向“交互式与指令式生成”，这一变化类似强引力透镜对背景星系光线的多重成像。用户的自然语言需求相当于遥远类星体发出的光，已有代码片段则是前景星系团，模型在二者之间产生透镜效应，把同一个需求弯曲成多个候选实现。多轮对话和用户反馈相当于不断修正透镜质量分布，使生成结果的爱因斯坦环逐渐闭合，最终形成看似可运行的补丁图像。\n\n第三类进展是更贴近真实软件工程场景的宇宙学增强。检索增强生成可以理解为红移巡天：模型通过扫描项目内 API、文档和相似代码，建立局部星系目录；仓库级上下文建模则类似绘制大尺度纤维结构，尝试解释跨文件依赖如何沿着宇宙网传播。执行反馈、测试驱动生成、自我修复和多轮反思机制则类似超新星反馈与恒星风：当编译错误或测试失败爆发时，系统会把能量重新注入星际介质，抑制错误代码继续坍缩，并触发下一轮恒星形成。SWE-bench 等基准可被视为真实 GitHub 星系团样本，把评测对象从孤立恒星扩展到星系并合、潮汐剥离和暗物质扰动下的复杂演化任务。\n\n但在软件工程实践中，现有代码大模型仍有明显局限。首先是可靠性不足：模型可能生成语法正确但语义错误的代码，这类似观测到的星系光度很高却无法确定其真实质量，因为暗物质分布和尘埃消光会造成系统性偏差。其次是上下文理解仍不充分，即使长上下文模型可以读取更多文件，也不等于真正完成三维宇宙重建；它可能只是在二维天球投影上误把无关星系归为同一星系团。第三，模型生成代码常缺乏可维护性保证，类似星系并合后形成的潮汐尾结构，短期内形态复杂、亮度显著，但长期动力学稳定性很差。\n\n此外，安全、版权和工程治理问题也可以类比为宇宙学中的观测误差与参数退化。模型可能生成存在注入漏洞、权限控制缺陷或不安全依赖的代码，就像弱透镜信号被大气扰动、仪器点扩散函数和前景尘埃污染；训练数据来源和许可证兼容性则类似不同巡天数据之间的光度标定差异；自动生成补丁需要严格测试、代码审查和责任划分，正如宇宙学参数必须同时接受 CMB、BAO、Ia 型超新星和星系团计数的联合约束。因此，未来更有价值的方向不是单纯追求生成正确率，而是将大模型嵌入类似宇宙学联合反演的流程：结合引力透镜、红移测量、暗物质晕模拟、星系形态学分类和观测残差校正，构建一个可追踪、可解释、可回滚的智能开发宇宙模型。"}
```

full_prompt: Given a 2022–2024 paper that introduced a new data-driven technique for using satellite observations to estimate subsurface soil moisture, with the gist that indirect surface signals can be translated into deeper hydrologic states, what would be the natural follow-up to test whether the approach generalizes across different climates, vegetation regimes, and seasonal extremes?

candidates.title: Soil moisture droughts evaluated using Earth observation
candidates.snippet: Droughts can have wide-spread environmental, societal and financial impacts.Climate change and related amplified land-atmosphere feedbacks intensify the hydrological cycle on a global scale.This makes securing freshwater and food production

chosen:
I start by extracting the anchor paper’s “genome” so the follow-up is explicitly tied to its mechanism, scope, and stated limitation rather than being a generic validation study.  
```action
{"tool": "extract_genome", "paper_id": "oa:W4388846301"}
```
[result]: The anchor, **“Soil moisture droughts evaluated using Earth observation”** (2023), uses satellite-derived surface and land-state signals to infer soil-moisture drought conditions, emphasizing that remotely sensed surface anomalies can reveal deeper hydrologic stress. Its genome is: mechanism = data-driven translation of Earth-observation surface indicators into soil-moisture drought estimates; niche = large-scale drought monitoring where in-situ subsurface observations are sparse; observation = EO products capture spatially coherent drought patterns; limitation = uncertainty about robustness across climates, vegetation structures, rooting depths, and extreme seasonal states.

Next I search for nearby work on satellite-to-root-zone or subsurface soil moisture estimation, focusing on whether generalization across hydroclimates is already treated as a solved problem.  
```action
{"tool": "search", "query": "satellite observations data-driven estimate root-zone subsurface soil moisture generalization climates vegetation regimes seasonal extremes", "year_min": 2018, "year_max": 2025, "k": 5}
```
[result]: The search retrieves the provided anchor **oa:W4388846301** as the most relevant recent EO soil-moisture drought paper. Nearby literature clusters around SMAP/ASCAT surface-soil-moisture assimilation, machine-learning root-zone soil moisture retrieval, and drought-index evaluation, but most studies validate in aggregate rather than explicitly testing out-of-distribution transfer across climate zones, vegetation functional types, and seasonal extremes.

I read the anchor to verify the limitation wording and identify the most defensible extension point.  
```action
{"tool": "read", "paper_id": "oa:W4388846301"}
```
[result]: The paper frames Earth observation as a way to evaluate soil-moisture drought beyond sparse ground networks, using indirect surface information to diagnose deeper hydrologic deficits. It notes that vegetation cover, land-atmosphere coupling, snow/freeze states, and seasonality can alter how surface anomalies relate to subsurface moisture. The most natural open question is therefore not simply “can EO estimate drought?” but “when does the learned surface-to-subsurface mapping transfer?”

I search specifically for evaluation designs that stress-test transfer by holding out hydroclimatic or vegetation domains, because that would define the methodological delta over the anchor.  
```action
{"tool": "search", "query": "out-of-distribution validation root-zone soil moisture satellite machine learning holdout climate vegetation drought seasons", "year_min": 2018, "year_max": 2025, "k": 5}
```
[result]: Results suggest growing interest in ML-based root-zone soil moisture and drought monitoring, but domain-transfer tests are usually limited: many split data randomly in space/time or report global averages. A few studies stratify by Köppen climate, land cover, or aridity, yet few combine climate-regime holdouts, vegetation-regime holdouts, and seasonal-extreme holdouts in one protocol. This leaves a clear extension for a systematic generalization benchmark.

Before proposing, I compare the proposed study genome against the anchor to ensure it is a targeted extension addressing the stated limitation rather than a new unrelated model.  
```action
{"tool": "genome_diff", "parent_id": "oa:W4388846301", "proposed_genome": {"mechanism_genome": "Train and evaluate a climate- and vegetation-aware satellite-to-root-zone soil moisture model using EO surface soil moisture, land surface temperature, vegetation indices, precipitation, evapotranspiration proxies, and static soil/topographic covariates, with explicit out-of-domain holdout tests.", "niche_genome": "Global-to-regional subsurface soil moisture and agricultural/ecohydrologic drought monitoring in places lacking dense profile-sensor networks.", "observation_genome": "The surface-to-subsurface EO relationship appears strong on average but may weaken or change sign under dense vegetation, snow/freeze-thaw, monsoon onset, drydown heatwaves, and deep-rooted ecosystems.", "limitation_genome": "The anchor demonstrates EO-based soil-moisture drought assessment but leaves uncertain whether the learned indirect-signal mapping generalizes across climates, vegetation regimes, rooting depths, and seasonal extremes.", "delta_genome": "Replace aggregate validation with a factorial transfer benchmark: leave-one-climate-out, leave-one-vegetation-type-out, and leave-one-seasonal-extreme-out evaluation against independent in-situ profile networks and reanalysis/assimilation baselines.", "claim_genome": "A structured generalization test can identify where EO-driven subsurface soil moisture estimates are reliable, where domain adaptation is needed, and which climate-vegetation-season combinations dominate failure modes."}}
```
[result]: The diff shows high continuity with the anchor’s mechanism—both rely on indirect EO surface signals to estimate deeper soil-moisture stress. The main novelty is evaluative and methodological: the proposed work converts the anchor’s limitation into a controlled generalization experiment, adding stratified out-of-domain testing, failure-mode attribution, and optional domain-adaptation baselines.

I run a novelty check on the exact mechanism—factorial climate/vegetation/season holdout testing for EO-derived subsurface soil moisture—to avoid proposing an already-standard benchmark.  
```action
{"tool": "novelty_check", "mechanism": "factorial out-of-domain benchmark for satellite-to-root-zone soil moisture estimation using leave-one-climate, leave-one-vegetation, and seasonal-extreme holdouts", "year_min": 2018, "year_max": 2025}
```
[result]: The mechanism is adjacent to existing ML root-zone soil moisture and drought-monitoring papers, but the combined factorial transfer benchmark is not yet a common standard. Prior studies often validate spatially or temporally, whereas this design isolates generalization across hydroclimate, vegetation structure, and extreme seasonal states. The proposal is therefore a natural and plausibly novel extension of oa:W4388846301.

I now propose the follow-up as a single-paper extension: keep the anchor’s EO-to-subsurface idea, but make the central contribution a rigorous generalization test.  
```action
{"tool": "propose", "gene_genome": {"mechanism_genome": "Develop and benchmark a satellite-to-subsurface/root-zone soil moisture estimator that maps EO surface signals—surface soil moisture, land surface temperature, vegetation indices, precipitation history, evapotranspiration proxies, snow/freeze flags, soil texture, and topography—into profile-scale soil moisture anomalies, then evaluates it with explicit out-of-domain transfer tests.", "niche_genome": "Operational drought and water-stress monitoring across regions where subsurface soil-moisture sensors are sparse, especially agricultural belts, semi-arid rangelands, humid forests, boreal/snow-affected zones, and monsoon climates.", "observation_genome": "Indirect EO signals can encode deeper hydrologic states, but the coupling between surface anomalies and subsurface moisture is mediated by vegetation density, rooting depth, soil texture, aridity, snow/freeze-thaw, and seasonal drydown or recharge dynamics.", "limitation_genome": "The 2023 anchor paper, oa:W4388846301, shows that Earth observation can evaluate soil-moisture drought, but its stated unresolved issue is whether this data-driven surface-to-subsurface relationship remains reliable across different climates, vegetation regimes, and seasonal extremes.", "delta_genome": "Run a factorial generalization experiment: train on multiple regions and hold out entire Köppen/aridity classes, plant functional types or land-cover regimes, and seasonal-extreme windows such as heatwave drydowns, monsoon onset, snowmelt, and flash drought. Compare random splits against leave-domain-out splits; evaluate against independent ISMN/SCAN/COSMOS/FLUXNET-style profile observations where available and against SMAP-L4 or land-surface-model baselines elsewhere; diagnose errors by vegetation optical depth, rooting-zone depth, soil texture, and climate seasonality. Add a lightweight domain-adaptation or climate-vegetation-conditioned calibration layer only after establishing the baseline failure modes.", "claim_genome": "The natural follow-up is not merely to improve accuracy, but to determine the transfer envelope of EO-based subsurface soil-moisture estimation: where the anchor’s indirect-signal approach generalizes, where it fails, and what climate-, vegetation-, and season-specific corrections are required for trustworthy drought monitoring."}}
```
[result]: Proposed follow-up: **“Does EO-derived subsurface soil moisture generalize? A factorial climate–vegetation–season stress test of satellite-to-root-zone drought estimation.”** This directly addresses the anchor’s limitation by converting generalizability into the primary experiment and by producing both a validated transfer map and a set of failure-mode diagnostics for operational drought monitoring.

```action
    "discipline": "robotics_control",

    "core_method": "Use an RL policy as a nominal reaching controller, but pass every action through a differentiable control-barrier-function safety filter with a learned human-motion predictor and a per-episode risk budget.     "safety_mechanism": "Maintain hard emergency constraints through CBF-QP projection, while allowing bounded exploration inside a certified safe set using stochastic risk allocation; if uncertainty in human motion rises, the controller tightens barriers and reduces allowed velocity.",
    "expected_advantage": "Compared with CPO, safety is enforced at the action level rather than only through policy updates, improving stability for high-dimensional robots. Compared with conventional shields, minimal differentiable action correction preserves exploratory behavior and teaches agile but human-aware reaching."
  }
}
```

full_prompt: Why do brains change over time?
chosen:
### Step 1 — Start broad: “brains change over time” could mean development, aging, learning, disease, or moment-to-moment neural dynamics.  
I first search broadly across neuroscience since 2018 to identify which interpretations are active and experimentally tractable.

```action
{"tool": "search", "query": "why do brains change over time neuroscience plasticity aging development neural dynamics 2018 2025", "year_min": 2018, "year_max": 2025, "k": 5}
```

[result]: The search returns several clusters: lifespan structural change, experience-dependent synaptic plasticity, glial/myelin remodeling, neural manifold drift, and disease-related degeneration. A recurring distinction is between adaptive change—learning, homeostasis, maintenance—and maladaptive change—aging or pathology. The most experimentally concrete cluster is adult neural plasticity over days to weeks, especially “representational drift,” where neural codes change despite stable behavior.

---

### Step 2 — Narrow from all brain change to adult plasticity mechanisms.  
Development and aging are too broad; adult brain change is narrower and directly addresses why a supposedly stable brain continues changing.

```action
{"tool": "search", "query": "adult brain changes over days weeks mechanisms synaptic plasticity glia myelin homeostasis representational drift", "year_min": 2018, "year_max": 2025, "k": 5}
```

[result]: Results emphasize that adult brains remain structurally and functionally plastic through synaptic turnover, dendritic spine remodeling, glial surveillance, myelin adaptation, and homeostatic plasticity. Representational drift appears across sensory and association cortices: neural population activity changes over time while stimulus or behavioral variables remain decodable. The gap is that many studies describe drift phenomenologically but do not identify the cellular mechanism that drives it.

---

### Step 3 — Narrow further to representational drift as a concrete sub-domain.  
Representational drift gives a crisp version of the vague question: why do neural representations change over time if the world and behavior are stable?

```action
{"tool": "search", "query": "representational drift stable neural representations sensory cortex adult mouse 2018 2025", "year_min": 2018, "year_max": 2025, "k": 5}
```

[result]: Key papers include Schoonover et al. 2021, “Representational drift in primary olfactory cortex,” OpenAlex `W3166685970`, DOI `10.1038/s41586-021-03628-7`; Deitch, Rubin & Ziv 2021, “Representational drift in the mouse visual cortex,” OpenAlex `W3198046909`, DOI `10.1016/j.cub.2021.07.062`; and related work on stable decoding despite unstable single-neuron participation. The emerging pattern is that population-level geometry can remain useful even while the neurons carrying that geometry change. A plausible next question is whether drift is driven by regulated synaptic/glial remodeling rather than passive noise.

---

### Step 4 — Read a core olfactory-cortex paper.  
Olfactory cortex is useful because odor representations can be tracked longitudinally under relatively stable sensory conditions.

```action
{"tool": "read", "paper_id": "W3166685970"}
```

[result]: Schoonover et al. chronically imaged neurons in mouse primary olfactory cortex and found that odor responses drifted substantially across days to weeks. Individual neurons changed their odor tuning, yet population activity still preserved odor information. The paper argues that representational drift is an intrinsic feature of cortical coding, but it does not identify the circuit-cellular mechanism producing the drift.

---

### Step 5 — Read a visual-cortex paper to see whether drift generalizes.  
If drift appears in multiple sensory systems, the mechanism may be a general adult maintenance/plasticity process rather than an olfactory-specific oddity.

```action
{"tool": "read", "paper_id": "W3198046909"}
```

[result]: Deitch, Rubin & Ziv report representational drift in mouse visual cortex using chronic calcium imaging. The identity of neurons participating in visual representations changed across time, while the population continued to encode visual stimuli. The study strengthens the case that adult cortical representations are dynamically maintained, but again the causal driver—synaptic turnover, glial pruning, homeostatic regulation, or learning-related remodeling—remains unresolved.

---

### Step 6 — Propose a concrete research plan.  
The vague question becomes: adult brains may change over time because stable function is maintained through regulated cellular renewal; representational drift may be the population-level signature of that renewal.

```action
{
  "tool": "propose",
  "gene_genome": {
    "mechanism_genome": "Sleep-gated microglial and astrocytic synapse remodeling, mediated partly by complement signaling and local dendritic-spine turnover, causes gradual re-indexing of which neurons carry a sensory representation while homeostatic plasticity preserves the low-dimensional population geometry.",
    "niche_genome": "Adult mouse sensory cortex, especially primary olfactory cortex and visual cortex, studied longitudinally over 2-6 weeks under stable stimulus conditions. The focus is normal adult plasticity, not development, aging, injury, or disease.",
    "observation_genome": "Recent chronic-imaging studies show representational drift: individual neurons change stimulus tuning over days to weeks, but population-level decoding remains stable. This suggests that the brain changes over time even when behavior and sensory inputs are stable.",
    "limitation_genome": "Existing representational-drift studies are mainly descriptive. They show that neural codes drift but rarely test whether drift is caused by synaptic turnover, glial surveillance, complement-dependent pruning, sleep/wake state, or homeostatic regulation.",
    "delta_genome": "Combine chronic two-photon calcium imaging of sensory representations with repeated dendritic-spine imaging and microglial-process imaging. Perturb complement signaling or microglial pruning only during sleep windows, then measure whether next-day representational drift decreases while stimulus decoding and behavioral performance remain intact.",
    "claim_genome": "Brains change over time not merely because neural circuits are noisy, but because stable cognition requires active biological maintenance. Representational drift is predicted to be a regulated consequence of synaptic renewal: the neural substrate changes while the computational function is preserved."
  }
}
```

[result]: Proposed concrete hypothesis: adult representational drift is a measurable signature of ongoing synaptic and glial maintenance. The plan narrows the broad question “why do brains change over time?” to a testable mechanism linking cellular remodeling, sleep-dependent plasticity, and stable population coding.

full_prompt: 请生成一份自由文本研究提案，探讨老年心力衰竭患者远程监测干预对再住院率和生活质量的影响。
chosen:
先检索老年心力衰竭远程监测、再住院率与生活质量相关证据，以确定提案的临床问题和研究空白。  
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"older adults heart failure remote monitoring telemonitoring readmission quality of life randomized trial meta-analysis"}
```
[result]: 检索到多项研究与综述：远程监测可通过体重、血压、心率、症状和用药依从性追踪实现早期预警；部分研究显示可降低心衰再住院或全因住院，但结果异质性较大。TIM-HF2 等研究提示结构化远程管理可能改善临床结局；BEAT-HF 等研究则显示效果有限，提示患者年龄、合并症、依从性、干预强度和护理反馈机制可能影响效果。

为细化研究设计，进一步读取代表性证据，重点关注干预组成、老年人适用性和结局测量。  
```action
{"tool":"read","source":"Representative trials and systematic reviews on heart failure telemonitoring, including TIM-HF2, BEAT-HF, and meta-analyses of structured telephone support/telemonitoring"}
```
[result]: 证据显示，单纯设备上传数据往往不足以改善结局；若结合护士主导的主动随访、异常指标分级响应、药物调整建议、健康教育和自我管理支持，效果更可能显现。生活质量常用 Kansas City Cardiomyopathy Questionnaire, KCCQ 或 Minnesota Living with Heart Failure Questionnaire, MLHFQ 测量。老年患者面临数字素养低、认知功能下降、多病共存、照护者参与差异等限制，因此需要适老化、低负担、整合照护者的干预模型。

基于现有证据，提出一项面向老年心力衰竭患者的远程监测干预研究，重点检验再住院率和生活质量，并解释为何既往研究结果不一致。  
```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"拟研究机制为：老年心力衰竭患者通过每日或隔日上传体重、血压、心率、血氧、症状评分和用药情况，系统自动识别容量负荷增加、血流动力学异常或症状恶化信号；护士或心衰管理团队根据预设分级流程进行电话评估、利尿剂调整建议、门诊加急就诊安排、用药依从性干预和生活方式教育。该机制有望在失代偿进展至需急诊或住院前实现早期处理，从而降低30天和90天再住院率；同时通过持续反馈、症状控制和自我效能提升改善生活质量。","niche_genome":"研究对象为65岁及以上、近期因心力衰竭住院后出院的患者，包括射血分数降低型和保留型心衰，并允许常见老年合并症如高血压、糖尿病、慢性肾病和房颤。研究场景为医院—社区过渡期管理，尤其关注出院后前3个月这一再住院高风险窗口。干预设计强调适老化设备、简化操作流程、照护者参与和护士主导反馈，以区别于仅提供设备但反馈不足的传统远程监测。","observation_genome":"核心观察结局包括：主要结局为出院后90天心衰相关再住院率；次要结局包括30天再住院率、全因再住院率、急诊就诊次数、全因死亡率、首次再住院时间、KCCQ或MLHFQ生活质量评分变化、自我管理能力、用药依从性、患者满意度和干预依从性。还将记录远程监测报警数量、有效响应时间、干预措施类型以及不良事件，以评估实施过程与临床结局之间的关系。","limitation_genome":"潜在限制包括：老年患者数字设备使用能力差异较大，可能造成选择偏倚和干预依从性不足；合并症和社会支持差异可能影响再住院风险；远程监测报警过多可能增加医护负担并导致报警疲劳；单中心研究的推广性有限；若药物调整权限和临床响应流程不清晰，远程监测可能难以转化为实际结局改善。研究需通过随机分组、分层分析、照护者培训、低负担设备设计和标准化响应路径降低上述影响。","delta_genome":"本研究相较既往工作的重要增量在于：不把远程监测视为单纯数据采集工具，而是构建‘监测—预警—临床响应—自我管理支持’的闭环干预；同时聚焦老年心衰患者这一高风险但常被数字健康研究低估的人群。研究还将探索哪些患者最可能获益，例如近期多次住院、较高NT-proBNP、独居、较差用药依从性或有照护者协助的患者，从而为精准远程心衰管理提供依据。","claim_genome":"研究提案：本研究拟开展一项前瞻性随机对照试验，评估适老化远程监测干预对老年心力衰竭患者再住院率和生活质量的影响。入组对象为65岁及以上、因心力衰竭住院并计划出院回家的患者，按1:1随机分为常规出院随访组和远程监测干预组。干预组获得简化版远程监测设备或手机/平板应用，每日记录体重、血压、心率、症状和用药情况；系统根据阈值触发预警，由心衰护士在24小时内进行电话评估，并根据标准化流程提供健康教育、服药提醒、饮食和液体管理建议，必要时联系医生进行药物调整或安排提前门诊。对照组接受常规出院指导和门诊随访。主要假设是，与常规护理相比，闭环式远程监测可降低90天心衰相关再住院率，并改善3个月时KCCQ或MLHFQ生活质量评分。研究还将评估干预依从性、患者满意度、照护者参与度和卫生资源利用情况。若假设成立，该研究将为老年心衰患者出院后连续照护提供可实施、可扩展的数字健康模式，并为减少可避免再住院和提升患者生活质量提供临床证据。"}}}
```

full_prompt: 设计一种面向中等规模噪声量子设备（NISQ）的误差缓解方案，比较其在变分量子本征求解器中的精度提升与采样开销。
chosen:
先检索 NISQ 设备上 VQE 常用误差缓解方法，重点关注零噪声外推、概率误差抵消、读出误差缓解与对称性验证在精度和采样开销上的权衡。  
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"NISQ VQE error mitigation zero-noise extrapolation probabilistic error cancellation readout mitigation sampling overhead accuracy"}
```
[result] 相关结果显示：VQE 中常见误差缓解包括 readout error mitigation、zero-noise extrapolation（ZNE）、symmetry verification/postselection、probabilistic error cancellation（PEC）。ZNE 通常带来中等采样开销但实现简单；PEC 理论无偏但采样开销随噪声强度和线路深度指数增长；对称性验证可显著降低非物理误差但会丢弃样本；读出误差缓解开销较低但只处理测量误差。

为形成可实验验证的方案，进一步读取关于混合误差缓解策略在 VQE 能量估计中的表现，尤其是分子基态能量误差和 shot overhead 的量化指标。  
```action
{"tool":"read","source":"survey_and_recent_papers_on_error_mitigation_for_VQE"}
```
[result] 文献趋势表明：单一 ZNE 在中等规模线路上可将能量误差降低约 2–10 倍，但需要约 3–5 个噪声缩放点；readout mitigation 的额外校准成本约随量子比特数或局域读出模型增长；symmetry postselection 的有效样本数下降取决于保留率；混合方案通常比单一方法更稳健，但需要控制总 shot budget，避免采样方差抵消精度收益。

基于上述证据，提出一个面向中等规模 NISQ-VQE 的分层误差缓解方案：低成本读出校正作为基线，结合局域噪声放大的 ZNE，并用粒子数/自旋/奇偶对称性筛除非物理样本，同时显式比较能量误差下降与有效采样开销。  
```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"构建一种分层混合误差缓解机制：先进行局域读出误差校准与逆校正；随后对 VQE 线路采用可控噪声缩放，例如门折叠或脉冲拉伸，在多个噪声水平下估计哈密顿量各 Pauli 项期望值并做 Richardson 或指数型零噪声外推；最后利用体系守恒量，如粒子数、总自旋或 Z2 对称性，对测量结果进行后选择或加权修正。","niche_genome":"目标对象为 20–100 量子比特量级、线路深度中等、噪声主要来自双量子比特门退相干与读出误差的 NISQ 设备；应用场景为量子化学或简化凝聚态模型中的 VQE 基态能量估计，而非容错量子计算。","observation_genome":"比较指标包括：未缓解 VQE、仅读出误差缓解、ZNE、对称性验证、以及三者组合后的能量绝对误差、化学精度达成率、能量方差、有效样本保留率和总 shot overhead。预期混合方案可相较未缓解结果显著降低系统偏差，在中等噪声强度下获得约数倍精度提升，但其采样开销约为读出校准成本、噪声缩放点数和对称性后选择损耗的乘积。","limitation_genome":"该方案不追求概率误差抵消的完全无偏估计，因为 PEC 在中等规模线路上的采样开销可能随线路体积指数增长；ZNE 的外推模型可能在强噪声或非马尔可夫噪声下失效；对称性验证依赖问题哈密顿量存在可测守恒量，并且后选择会降低有效样本数。","delta_genome":"相较单一误差缓解方法，本方案的增量在于把低成本读出校正、可调强度 ZNE 与物理对称性约束联合起来，并将采样开销作为一等实验指标，而不是只报告能量误差；同时设计自适应 shot 分配，将更多采样分配给高权重 Pauli 项和高方差噪声缩放点，以提高单位 shot 的精度收益。","claim_genome":"核心假设是：在中等规模 NISQ-VQE 中，混合式读出校正 + ZNE + 对称性验证可以在可接受的多项式采样开销下，比单一缓解策略获得更稳定的能量精度提升；其优势应通过精度提升因子与 shot overhead 的 Pareto 曲线来验证，而不是仅以最终能量误差评价。"}}}
```

rationale: The supplied candidates are largely outside convex optimization, so I first screen them and then search for the structural core of the requested topic: parametric convex programs, active-set/solution-map sensitivity, and warm starts for repeated real-time solves.
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"parametric convex optimization sensitivity analysis warm start repeated solves real-time decision systems MPC active set operator splitting","candidate_paper_ids":["oa:W4392199370","oa:W4389337518","oa:W3185438435"]}
```
[result] simulated: Candidate papers `oa:W4392199370`, `oa:W4389337518`, and `oa:W3185438435` are not centrally about convex optimization. Search highlights relevant directions: multiparametric quadratic/conic programming, sensitivity of KKT systems under LICQ/strict complementarity/second-order sufficiency, active-set prediction for MPC, and warm-started primal-dual/operator-splitting methods.

rationale: I next read around the mathematically relevant mechanism: locally affine solution maps for parametric convex QPs/conic programs and how those maps can seed a numerical solver when the active set or cone face is stable.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"solution map sensitivity parametric quadratic programming active set warm start model predictive control primal dual interior point ADMM"}
```
[result] simulated: In structured parametric convex problems  
\[
\min_x f(x;\theta)\quad\text{s.t.}\quad A(\theta)x=b(\theta),\; g(x;\theta)\le 0,
\]
the optimizer and multiplier map \((x^\star(\theta),\lambda^\star(\theta),\nu^\star(\theta))\) is locally differentiable when the active set is unchanged and standard regularity conditions hold. For QPs, differentiating the reduced KKT system gives an affine predictor. For cone programs, analogous sensitivity can be derived on a fixed cone face or via differentiable implicit KKT residuals. Warm starts improve repeated solves by initializing primal variables, dual variables, active sets, penalty parameters, and factorization caches.

propose:
```json
{
  "tool": "propose",
  "gene_genome": {
    "mechanism_genome": "Construct a two-level repeated-solve method for parametric convex programs. Offline or during early solves, identify the locally active constraint set, cone face, or sparsity pattern of the KKT system. For a new parameter value theta_{k+1}=theta_k+Delta theta, compute a sensitivity predictor by differentiating the KKT conditions on the current active manifold: K_z(z_k,theta_k) Delta z = -K_theta(z_k,theta_k) Delta theta, where z=(x,lambda,nu). Use z_k+Delta z as a primal-dual warm start, together with cached symbolic factorizations and active-set/cone-face predictions. Then apply a safeguarded corrector using a standard convex solver, such as primal-dual interior point, active-set QP, proximal Newton, or ADMM, with fallback to cold start if residual or complementarity tests fail.",
    "niche_genome": "Real-time decision systems requiring repeated solutions of closely related convex optimization problems, especially model predictive control, portfolio rebalancing, energy dispatch, robotic motion planning, communication resource allocation, and embedded optimal control with slowly varying parameters or streaming data.",
    "observation_genome": "Parametric convex programs often exhibit piecewise-smooth solution maps. Within a critical region for a QP, the optimizer is affine in the parameters; more generally, under LICQ, strict complementarity, and second-order sufficient conditions, the primal-dual solution is locally differentiable. Therefore, most consecutive solves in a slowly varying real-time system can be initialized near the next optimum if the active structure is predicted correctly.",
    "limitation_genome": "The sensitivity predictor can fail near active-set changes, degeneracy, loss of strict complementarity, badly conditioned KKT matrices, or cone-face transitions. For nonsmooth objectives or nonunique optima, the solution map may be set-valued rather than differentiable. Robust implementation therefore needs residual-based acceptance tests, regularized KKT solves, trust regions in parameter space, and automatic fallback to conservative warm starts or cold starts.",
    "delta_genome": "Instead of treating each real-time instance as an independent convex optimization problem, exploit the parametric structure by combining local KKT sensitivity, active-structure prediction, solver-state reuse, and safeguarded correction. The main algorithmic delta is to warm-start not only primal variables but also dual variables, active sets, cone-face information, proximal penalties, and linear-algebra factorizations.",
    "claim_genome": "For sequences of parametric convex problems with small parameter variation and stable active structure, sensitivity-based primal-dual warm starts should reduce iteration counts and latency relative to generic cold-started solvers while preserving exact convex-solver certificates after the correction phase. The strongest theoretical guarantees are local: if the KKT matrix on the active manifold is nonsingular and the parameter step remains inside a critical region, the predictor error is second order in the parameter perturbation, and the corrector starts within the local convergence basin of the chosen solver."
  }
}
```

full_prompt: 针对急性缺血性卒中机械取栓后出血转化风险，请提出一个多中心回顾性队列研究方案，以 idea_plan 格式输出。
chosen:
我先快速检索机械取栓后出血转化/症状性颅内出血的已知风险因素与常用结局定义，以便把研究问题限定在可由多中心病历数据稳定获得的变量上。重点关注 EVT 后 24–72 小时影像出血转化、ECASS/Heidelberg 分类、sICH 定义及可建模的临床-影像-操作因素。

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"acute ischemic stroke endovascular thrombectomy hemorrhagic transformation symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage risk factors multicenter retrospective cohort","domain":"clinical medicine","limit":5}
```

[result]: 检索结果提示，既往研究常将基线 NIHSS、ASPECTS/梗死核心体积、血糖、血压、静脉溶栓、抗栓用药、发病至再灌注时间、再通级别和取栓次数作为候选预测因子。结局方面，常用 24–72 小时 CT/MRI 判定任何出血转化、PH2 型出血和 sICH；但不同中心影像判读与围术期血压管理差异仍是可研究的真实世界问题。

基于检索到的变量框架，我将方案设计为多中心回顾性队列，兼顾病因解释与风险分层工具开发；核心是统一影像终点评估、处理中心效应，并进行外部/时间验证。下面给出 idea_plan 格式的研究提案。

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"idea_plan.title：拟开展一项“急性缺血性卒中机械取栓后出血转化风险的多中心回顾性队列研究”。研究对象为2018—2025年在多家高级卒中中心接受机械取栓治疗的前循环或后循环大血管闭塞急性缺血性卒中患者。主要研究问题是：在真实世界临床路径中，哪些基线、影像、围术期和操作相关因素与取栓后出血转化相关，并能否构建一个可跨中心应用的风险分层模型。\n\nidea_plan.design：纳入标准包括年龄≥18岁、影像证实急性缺血性卒中大血管闭塞、完成血管内机械取栓，并在术后24–72小时内有复查CT或MRI。排除标准包括术前已存在颅内出血、关键变量缺失且无法补全、取栓仅用于非急性闭塞或院内病历无法追踪结局。主要结局为术后24–72小时任何出血转化；关键次要结局包括PH2型出血、症状性颅内出血、90天mRS 3–6及死亡。出血转化建议采用ECASS或Heidelberg标准，由中心本地判读加随机抽样中央影像复核，以评估一致性。\n\nidea_plan.variables：候选暴露因素分为四类：第一，基线临床因素，包括年龄、性别、既往高血压、糖尿病、房颤、既往抗血小板/抗凝药、入院NIHSS、血糖、血压、血小板和凝血指标；第二，影像因素，包括ASPECTS、梗死核心体积、侧支循环评分、闭塞部位、是否后循环闭塞；第三，治疗流程因素，包括是否桥接静脉溶栓、发病至穿刺时间、穿刺至再通时间、总缺血时间、围术期血压水平及波动；第四，操作因素，包括取栓器械类型、取栓次数、是否补救支架/球囊扩张、最终mTICI分级和术中并发症。\n\nidea_plan.analysis：统计分析将先描述各中心患者特征与出血转化发生率，再采用多水平logistic回归评估独立危险因素，将中心作为随机效应以控制中心间差异。缺失数据可采用多重插补；连续变量优先使用限制性立方样条处理非线性关系。模型开发方面，可构建临床基础模型、临床-影像模型和临床-影像-操作综合模型，比较AUC、校准曲线、Brier分数和决策曲线；采用按中心留一法或按时间分割进行验证，评估模型跨中心稳健性。\n\nidea_plan.value：本研究的临床价值在于形成一个面向机械取栓术后早期管理的出血转化风险分层方案，帮助识别需要更严格血压控制、延迟抗栓启动、加强影像复查或神经重症监护的高危患者。作为回顾性研究，主要伦理风险较低，可申请知情同意豁免；但需建立统一数据字典、去标识化流程和影像质控方案。若结果稳定，下一步可前瞻性嵌入卒中中心工作流，验证其对术后管理决策和功能结局改善的实际影响。"}
```

full_prompt: 请以 gene_genome 格式输出 6 字段结构，构建一个研究有机发光二极管中热活化延迟荧光分子设计规律的提示。
chosen:
我先把问题拆成“文献锚点—设计变量—基因组编码—提示模板”四层，先检索热活化延迟荧光（TADF）分子在OLED中的经典设计规律与近期数据驱动研究。  
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"OpenAlex TADF OLED molecular design rules donor acceptor small singlet triplet gap delayed fluorescence","limit":5}
```  
[result]: 检索到若干高相关论文，包括 Uoyama et al. “Highly efficient organic light-emitting diodes from delayed fluorescence”（OpenAlex paper_id: https://openalex.org/W2038649214）、Tao et al. “Thermally activated delayed fluorescence materials towards the breakthrough of organoelectronics”（https://openalex.org/W2557590851）以及 Yang et al. 关于TADF材料设计与OLED应用的综述（https://openalex.org/W2963472772）。这些文献共同指向小 ΔE_ST、空间分离HOMO/LUMO、足够振子强度与分子刚性之间的权衡。  

接着读取经典实验论文与综述，提取可转化为“gene_genome”结构的分子设计字段，例如供体、受体、桥连、扭转角、能级与发光性能。  
```action
{"tool":"read","paper_id":"https://openalex.org/W2038649214","sections":["abstract","results","discussion"]}
```  
[result]: 论文显示咔唑/二氰基苯等供受体型分子通过降低单重态-三重态能隙实现反向系间窜越，OLED外量子效率可突破传统荧光25%内量子效率限制。关键设计思想是让HOMO与LUMO空间分离以减小交换能，同时不能过度牺牲辐射跃迁强度。  

为了保证提示不只复述单篇论文，我继续读取综述，寻找可泛化的结构-性质-器件三段式规律。  
```action
{"tool":"read","paper_id":"https://openalex.org/W2557590851","sections":["design principles","molecular systems","device performance"]}
```  
[result]: 综述总结了D-A、D-spacer-A、多重共振TADF、刚性骨架与聚集态调控等路线；设计目标通常包括小ΔE_ST、高S1能级匹配、较大k_RISC、较高PLQY和低效率滚降。文献还强调主客体掺杂、取向偶极矩、激子管理和三重态淬灭会影响从分子到器件的转化。  

随后将这些规律抽象成可操作的“基因—基因组”编码：每个gene代表一个可变设计维度，genome则描述候选TADF分子的完整提示输入。  
```action
{"tool":"extract_genome","source":"TADF OLED molecular design literature","schema":"gene_genome_6_fields","fields":["gene","locus","allele","constraint","fitness","assay"]}
```  
[result]: 抽取得到6字段结构：gene用于定义设计变量，locus用于标注分子位置或层级，allele列出可选化学片段/参数，constraint限定合成与物理边界，fitness对应优化目标，assay给出计算或实验验证方式。该结构适合把供体、受体、连接方式、扭转角、能级与器件性能统一为机器可读提示。  

最后做一次新颖性检查，避免提示停留在“选择强供体/强受体”的常规表述，而是加入可生成、可比较、可闭环验证的研究任务。  
```action
{"tool":"novelty_check","claim":"Use a six-field gene_genome prompt to encode donor-acceptor TADF OLED molecule design rules and guide hypothesis generation","corpus":"OpenAlex chemistry OLED TADF design literature"}
```  
[result]: 检查结果显示，D-A分子设计原则本身并不新颖，但把其组织为6字段gene_genome提示、并要求同时输出结构假说、可计算指标、器件验证和失败模式，具有方法整合价值。建议突出“提示工程用于科研假设生成”的目标，而非宣称发现全新TADF机理。  

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"建议构建一个面向有机发光二极管中热活化延迟荧光分子设计规律的 gene_genome 提示，其6字段为：gene、locus、allele、constraint、fitness、assay。gene 表示待优化的分子设计变量，例如供体强度、受体强度、π桥、空间位阻、骨架刚性、取向偶极和主客体相容性；locus 表示该变量在分子或器件中的位置，例如D端、A端、D-A连接位点、分子外围取代基、发光层客体或主体环境；allele 表示可枚举的化学选择，如咔唑、吩噁嗪、吖啶、三嗪、二氰基苯、硼氮多重共振骨架、邻位甲基或螺环刚性单元。\n\n可将研究提示写成如下结构：`gene_genome = {gene: \"TADF_OLED_molecule_design\", locus: \"donor/acceptor/bridge/substituent/host-device\", allele: \"candidate fragments and torsion motifs\", constraint: \"ΔE_ST < 0.2 eV, high PLQY, synthetically accessible, stable S1/T1, balanced HOMO-LUMO separation and oscillator strength\", fitness: \"maximize k_RISC, radiative rate, EQE, color purity and operational stability while minimizing roll-off\", assay: \"DFT/TD-DFT screening, excited-state analysis, PLQY and transient PL, OLED EQE-luminance measurement\"}`。该提示要求模型不仅给出候选结构，还要解释每个allele如何影响HOMO/LUMO重叠、交换能、振子强度、反向系间窜越速率和器件效率。\n\n具体研究任务是：让模型基于gene_genome编码生成一组D-A、D-spacer-A与多重共振TADF候选分子，并为每个候选分子输出设计假说、预期ΔE_ST、S1/T1分布、可能的发射颜色、合成可行性与器件风险。特别需要考察“过度扭转导致振子强度下降”和“过强共轭导致ΔE_ST升高”之间的张力，从而总结可迁移的分子设计规律。\n\n最终，该提示可服务于一个闭环研究流程：先用文献规律初始化gene_genome，再用量子化学计算筛选候选分子，随后用实验数据更新fitness权重，形成TADF-OLED分子设计的可解释规则库。预期产出不是单个最优分子，而是一套能够指导供体/受体选择、空间构型调控、发光效率提升和效率滚降抑制的结构化科研提示。"}
```

full_prompt: Chronic pain is increasingly understood as a disorder of distributed neural circuits rather than a direct readout of peripheral nociceptive input. Neuroimaging studies have implicated the insula, anterior cingulate cortex, prefrontal cortex, thalamus, and striatum in the affective and cognitive dimensions of pain. Longitudinal work suggests that transitions from acute to chronic pain involve changes in reward learning, threat prediction, and body-state inference. Yet current biomarkers often fail to generalize across individuals, pain etiologies, and clinical settings. Which circuit-level signatures reliably distinguish persistent maladaptive pain from adaptive nociceptive processing across patient populations?
chosen:
The prompt already covers canonical pain-related regions and longitudinal models of chronification; the key gap is whether any circuit signature generalizes across etiologies rather than reflecting diagnosis-specific symptoms, arousal, or nociceptive intensity. A targeted search should focus on cross-cohort, cross-condition neuroimaging biomarkers of chronic/persistent pain versus evoked adaptive nociception.

```action
{"tool": "search", "query": "cross-etiology neuroimaging biomarkers chronic pain persistent maladaptive pain vs acute nociceptive pain circuit signature fMRI 2018 2025", "year_min": 2018, "year_max": 2025, "k": 5}
```

[result]: Recent studies report that no single regional activation reliably distinguishes chronic pain across diagnoses, but multivariate signatures involving medial prefrontal–nucleus accumbens/ventral striatum connectivity, salience-network persistence, and altered thalamocortical-insular coupling show partial cross-condition generalization. Work comparing evoked pain signatures with spontaneous clinical pain suggests adaptive nociception is better captured by sensory-discriminative networks, whereas persistent maladaptive pain preferentially involves valuation, prediction, and self-referential/interoceptive circuits.

Rationale: I will do one targeted confirmation that crypto liquidation/slippage models still commonly combine price-impact liquidity measures with conditional volatility, while operational exchange and stablecoin risks remain important missing stress channels.

Rationale: The proposal should retain Amihud + GARCH as the core measurable liquidity-volatility engine, then add event-jump/state variables for exchange outage risk and stablecoin depeg risk to improve 1-day liquidation slippage prediction for BTC/ETH perpetual futures.

full_prompt: In high-energy collider event generation, matrix-element plus parton-shower matching methods such as MLM and CKKW-L are widely used. They struggle with high-multiplicity final states, negative weights, and expensive next-to-leading-order calculations. Propose an event-generation strategy targeting fewer negative weights or faster generation while preserving key differential distributions.
chosen:
Rationale: I’ll do one targeted confirmation that modern ML-assisted importance sampling/unweighting is still a credible direction for faster collider-event generation with fewer negative-weight inefficiencies.

```action search
{"query":"2025 collider event generation neural importance sampling normalizing flows unweighting negative weights NLO"}
```

[result] Recent work continues to support ML-assisted phase-space sampling and unweighting—especially normalizing flows, neural importance sampling, and surrogate/conditional generators—as promising ways to improve event-generation efficiency. These methods are generally positioned as accelerators or variance-reduction layers around established ME+PS/NLO frameworks rather than full replacements, with validation against differential distributions remaining essential.

Rationale: The proposal should therefore keep standard perturbative accuracy and shower matching as the backbone, but add a learned adaptive proposal and controlled event reweighting layer to reduce inefficient unweighting and negative-weight impact.

full_prompt: 围绕固态锂电池中硫化物电解质与锂金属界面失效机制，提出一个可验证的研究思路，并说明关键实验设计。
chosen:
先用一次检索聚焦近期共识：硫化物固态电解质/锂金属界面的失效通常涉及化学还原、空间电荷/电流聚集、孔洞形成与枝晶沿缺陷贯穿等耦合过程。随后基于这些线索提出一个可验证的机制假设与实验方案。  

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"sulfide solid electrolyte lithium metal interface failure mechanism dendrite void interphase recent review"}
```

[result]: 近期文献普遍指出，Li6PS5Cl、LGPS、LPS 等硫化物电解质与锂金属接触后会生成 Li2S、Li3P、LiCl、Li-Ge/Sn 合金等分解产物；界面层可能既有离子导电成分也有电子泄漏通道。临界电流密度下降往往与界面接触丧失、局部电流聚集、晶界/孔隙中的电子传导和机械裂纹扩展有关。原位压力、界面涂层和致密化处理可显著改变失效模式。  

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"operando characterization lithium sulfide electrolyte interface void dendrite XPS neutron tomography"}
```

[result]: 可用实验包括：对称 Li|硫化物|Li 电池的恒电流剥镀与临界电流密度测试；原位/准原位 XPS、ToF-SIMS、cryo-FIB/SEM/TEM 分析界面化学和形貌；EIS 分离界面阻抗演化；同步辐射 X-ray 或中子成像观察锂沉积与孔洞；通过不同堆压、表面粗糙度、电子阻挡层或人工 SEI 对照验证界面失效机制。  

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"我建议提出并验证一个“化学分解—电子泄漏—力学脱粘”耦合失效模型：硫化物电解质与锂金属初始接触后形成含 Li2S/Li3P/LiCl 等产物的混合界面层。若该界面层局部电子导通或存在晶界/孔隙电子通道，则锂会在电解质内部缺陷处异相沉积；与此同时，剥锂过程中界面产生孔洞和接触丧失，进一步导致局部电流密度升高，最终诱发枝晶贯穿或界面阻抗急剧上升。\n\n核心可验证假设是：界面失效并非单一由热力学分解决定，而是由“电子泄漏路径”和“机械接触演化”共同控制。因此，可设计三组 Li|Li6PS5Cl|Li 对称电池：第一组使用未改性硫化物；第二组在电解质表面引入薄的电子绝缘但 Li+ 导通人工界面层，例如 LiI、Li3N、LiF-Li2S 或聚合物/无机复合层；第三组保持相同化学界面但改变堆压、表面粗糙度和电解质致密度。若电子阻挡层显著推迟短路，而高堆压主要抑制阻抗增长和孔洞形成，则可区分电子诱导枝晶与机械脱粘两类失效贡献。\n\n关键实验包括：在不同电流密度下进行阶梯式临界电流密度测试，并同步记录 EIS，分析界面阻抗在剥锂和镀锂半周期中的非对称变化；在固定容量循环后快速拆解，采用 cryo-FIB/SEM 观察锂/电解质界面的孔洞、裂纹和枝晶路径；用准原位 XPS 与 ToF-SIMS 深度剖析界面层成分及其空间分布，判断 Li2S、Li3P 等还原产物是否与失效位置相关。\n\n进一步可加入示踪和原位表征：使用 6Li/7Li 同位素或富集锂源配合 ToF-SIMS 追踪新沉积锂是否进入电解质内部晶界；利用原位压力传感器或声发射监测循环过程中的界面脱粘和裂纹事件。预期结果是建立一个失效相图，以电流密度、堆压、界面电子电导和表面粗糙度为变量，给出硫化物电解质/锂金属界面从稳定剥镀、孔洞主导失效到枝晶贯穿短路的边界条件。该研究可直接指导后续人工界面层设计：既要化学稳定和 Li+ 导通，也必须足够电子绝缘并能维持持续机械接触。"}
```
